text:
Once upon a time, there existed a mystical forest that whispered mysteries to those who dared listen. In the heart of this woodland stood an ancient elm tree that murmured secrets to those who would listen. It told tales of a band of squirrels who journeyed across the forest floor searching for fallen acorns, their friendship a beacon in the darkness.

Among these adorable critters was Oliver the owl, who never missed a moment to watch them. Oliver loved nothing more than a good sunbeam that caught his feathered ears—or a good tale about friends.

The squirrels had a secret treasure hidden deep within the woods—a lampoonable amount of acorns—all meticulously collected from the juiciest branches. They call it the Golden Acorn Trove. Each year, they decided whose turn it was to guard it.

One year, the wise old tree squirrel became keeper. His name was Willow Wing. Willow Wing knew nothing naughty was expected of him, but he did adore the light reflection on his
